feedback.text: Im all for the more customer friendly market and us getting to keep digital products we pay for and use, but can we please also include videogames in there? They are still a digital product that we pay for/use and a massive portion of them have a really high chance to just stop working forever due to their publishers not wanting to give access to the communities of said games to maintain and preserve the game they love as well as the hard work of the developers and artists responsible for said videogames. A recent personal example I have had the displeasure of experiencing is a videogame called Anthem, developed by Bioware and published by EA. It was meant to be a live-service (online and improved over time with the input of the community) but due to greed and many other factors the game was released in a poor state, the developers wanted to improve that game and give us what was promised to us. Later down the line the publisher decided to move all the developers working on the game, trying to make it better to then work on other projects because the game wasnt making them enough money, putting it on "live support" with only the servers being maintained with no hopes of any future updates. Later, in January 2026 the servers of that game will be shut down, rendering the game unplayable because of the way it was made, they didnt even try to give us tools to preserve it and all of its cool ideas, great environment and phenomenal movement will be deleted from all of us who bought the game with no refunds nor compensations. Removing access to those who want to experience it for what it could have been and those who want to learn from its mistakes and triumphs. All this is just one case of many, the current state of the game industry is filled to the brim with great games with uncertain future if they are even going to be able to be played even a year after its being purchased and I want it to improve, change for the benefit of the consumers and the people who care about its success and potential. Thank you for reading and please consider this as an opportunity to include videogames in there which are loved by so many of us
